notes: Roman usurper emperor of the Gallic Empire (r. 260-269), known for establishing independent rule in Gaul and claiming titles such as Restitutor Orbis.

docs.title: Life of Aurelian
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: His successes were commemorated by the official assumption of the title Restitutor Orbis , which appears in inscriptions and on coins; the latter bear also the titles Pacator Orbis , Restitutor Saeculi , Restitutor Gentis ,

docs.title: Life of Tyranni Triginta
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: Postumus 3 This man,⁠ 5 most valiant in war and most steadfast in peace, was so highly respected for his whole manner of life that he was even entrusted by Gallienus with the care of his son Saloninus (whom he had placed in command of Gaul) as the guardian of his life and conduct and his
